Off Calendar Dec-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. RUSSELL LEIBSON, TEL. NO. 543-4488 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WILLIAM WOLF GWIRE, TEL. NO. 296-8880 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT

RULING - A R G U E D;THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANT BELLAIRE TOWER HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Requests For Admission, And Form Interrogatories Or Alternatively, To Strike Cross-Complaint; And For Sanction - Motion to compel further responses to Request for Production of Documents is Granted. As to Requests that Golden State has trouble understanding, the response should speak to documents which would fall within the category as can be understood, especially in light of Bellaire's explanations in the Separate Statement. C.C.P. 2031.220 & 2031.240. Those documents must be produced. Motion to compel further responses to Request for Admission Nos. 1, 11, 13-21 is granted. Golden State shall admit, deny or explain why it can neither admit or deny the Requests without objection. As to Request for Admission No. 22, the motion is denied. Holquin v. Sup. Ct. (Hoage) (1972) 22 Cal.App.3d 812, 820.
Motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatories Nos. 1.1, 3.3, 3.6, 3.7, 9.1(b)&(c), 9.2, 12.1, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.7, 15.1, 17.1 & 50.2 is granted. Complete answers without objections shall be provided, except with respect to 12.3 if Golden State can "make a preliminary or foundational showing that answering the interrogatory would reveal the attorney's tactics, impressions, or evaluation of the case, or would result in opposing counsel taking undue advantage of the attorney's industry or efforts." Coito v. Superior Court (State) (2012) 54 Cal.4th 480, 502. The motion is denied with respect to No. 12.2.
All further responses and documents shall be delivered to and received by the Law Offices of Russell Leibson no later than January 6, 2014. No sanctions are awarded. If trial date is continued, then the responses and documents are due 1/22/14. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: David May; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported.
